title: polygala amara subsp. amarella
intro: Polygala amara subsp. amarella, also known as bitter milkwort or bitter polygala, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the milkwort family (Polygalaceae). This plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its purported medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Polygala amara subsp. amarella include its potential to lower blood sugar levels, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. In herbal preparations, the plant is often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
